hooks.command: FILE=$(cat | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // empty'); if [ -n "$FILE" ] && echo "$FILE" | grep -qE '\.md$'; then npx markdownlint-cli2 --fix "$FILE" 2>/dev/null; fi

hooks.prompt: If stop_hook_active is true in the input, respond {"ok": true} immediately. Otherwise: Check if the assistant's response completed all tasks the user requested. Look at the todo list if present — are there pending or in-progress items? Did the assistant leave any work unfinished or promise to do something it didn't do? If everything is done, respond {"ok": true}. If work remains, respond {"ok": false, "reason": "Brief description of what's incomplete"}.

hooks.command: osascript -e 'display notification "Claude Code needs your attention" with title "Claude Code"'
